book_claims.mechanism: Ancestral food principle: eat foods common in your family's dietary heritage to minimize intolerance risk
book_claims.text: Asparagus is a traditional staple of the German diet and recommended for those with German ancestry as a safe ancestral food

book_claims.mechanism: Excellent folate source; supports DNA methylation and cardiovascular health
book_claims.text: High folate content: 89 mcg per 4 spears boiled (22% DV); paired with salmon in the Longevity Diet

supplementary_research.finding: Asparagus contains inulin-type fructans (prebiotic fiber) that stimulate beneficial Bifidobacterium; supports gut microbiome health relevant to longevity
Asparagus folate bioavailability ~50–70%; cooking in water reduces folate by ~30%, steaming preferred to preserve content

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: ~50–70% bioavailability; steam rather than boil to reduce losses
Fermented by gut bacteria; promotes Bifidobacterium growth
Fat-soluble; take with olive oil for optimal absorption

synergies.note: Longevity Diet pairing; omega-3 from salmon enhances fat-soluble vitamin absorption from asparagus
Fat improves vitamin K and other fat-soluble nutrient absorption; traditional dressing pairing
Vitamin C in lemon enhances non-heme iron absorption; brightens bitter notes
